calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-12-2022 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2021 Set For Trial On Feb-14-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JASON M. SKAGGS, Esq. of Skaggs Faucette LLP (Tel: 650-617-3226) for Plaintiff Zscaler, Inc.;
Atty. VALERIE M. WAGNER, Esq. of GCA Law Partners LLP (Tel: 650-428-3900) for Defendant
George Symons.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GEORGE SYMONS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer is overruled.

"[T]o support a cause of action for fraud by concealment a plaintiff must show (1) concealment of a material fact by the defendant; (2) the defendant had a duty to disclose that fact to the plaintiff; (3) the defendant concealed the fact with an intent to induce reliance by the plaintiff; (4) the plaintiff was unaware of the fact and would not have acted as he or she did with knowledge of the concealed fact; and (5) the plaintiff suffered injury." (Doe v. Superior Court (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 239, 244.) To survive a demurrer, allegations of fraud must be pled with specificity. (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) However, less specificity is required in the context of negative fraud or "when it appears from the nature of the allegations that the defendant must necessarily possess full information concerning the facts of the controversy . . . ." (See Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

The complaint sufficiently alleges a duty to disclose. "There are four circumstances in which nondisclosure or concealment may constitute actionable fraud: (1) when the defendant is in a fiduciary relationship with the plaintiff; (2) when the defendant had exclusive knowledge of material facts not known to the plaintiff; (3) when the defendant actively conceals a material fact from the plaintiff; and (4) when the defendant makes partial representations but also suppresses some material facts." (OCM Principal Opportunities Fund, L.P. v. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 835, 859.) "[D]uty of disclosure may exist when one party to a transaction has sole knowledge or access to material facts and knows that such facts are not known to the other party. Thus, a duty to disclose may arise from the relationship between seller and buyer, employer and prospective employee, doctor and patient, or parties entering into any kind of contractual agreement." (LiMandri v. Judkins (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 326, 337.) The complaint's allegations are sufficient. "Symons had a duty to disclose these facts to Zscaler. That duty arose from his involvement in the negotiations with Zscaler and his status as a point of contact for Zscaler (including as the signer of the Agreement and the Order Documents) and Zscaler's contractual relationship with his company, NSS, as well as Symons exclusive knowledge of these facts that were not known to Zscaler." (Compl., Para. 12.) The complaint sufficiently alleges reliance. "Zscaler was unaware of these facts and certainly would not have paid $560,000 to NSS through NSS's Silicon Valley Bank account if it had known of these facts." (Compl., Para. 13.) The complaint also sufficiently alleges damages. "As a result of Symons' concealment or suppression of these facts, Zscaler paid $560,000 on September 3, 2020, and has not been able to recover any of that amount from NSS or Silicon Valley Bank, and therefore has suffered damages in that amount as a result of Symons' fraudulent concealment." (Compl., Para. 14.) Defendant's arguments on reply go beyond the scope of a demurrer, and challenge the truthfulness of the allegations. For the purposes of a demurrer, allegations in a complaint are accepted as true, "however improbable they may be." (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
